tipRules.tip: KVD: 'On natural lakes in early fall, use a crankbait first to cover water and identify key spots. Then switch to a deep jerkbait to triple catch rates — it stays in the strike zone longer and matches the conditions better. Target inside turns cutting into breaklines, not just points.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'When fishing expansive flats with breaks, mark waypoints at each catch. The pattern often isn't the obvious points — it's tapering breaks with inside turns cutting into the breakline. Those inside turns concentrate fish. Use a crankbait to survey, then switch to a jerkbait to work the productive spots more efficiently.'
